title: NSE Option Pricing & VaR Dashboard
description: Interactive Streamlit dashboard for pricing options on NSE-listed equities using Black-Scholes and Monte Carlo simulation. Includes historical VaR and CVaR computation, implied volatility surface visualization, and Greeks sensitivity analysis. Designed for real-time risk monitoring of equity derivatives on the Nairobi Securities Exchange.
longDescription: Built a comprehensive risk analytics platform targeting the Kenyan derivatives market. The system implements Black-Scholes closed-form pricing, binomial trees, and 10,000-path Monte Carlo simulations. Value-at-Risk is computed at 95% and 99% confidence levels using both historical simulation and parametric methods. CVaR (Expected Shortfall) is reported alongside for tail-risk quantification.

title: Agricultural Credit Scoring Model
description: XGBoost-based credit risk model achieving 89% accuracy across 500+ smallholder farmers. Integrates agricultural metrics, seasonal cash-flow patterns, and mobile money transaction history. SHAP explainability layer ensures regulatory transparency for fintech deployment.
longDescription: Developed for Sowphie's agri-fintech platform to automate credit underwriting for smallholder farmers lacking traditional credit history. The pipeline ingests multi-source data (satellite yield proxies, mobile money history, farm size), engineers 40+ features, and trains an XGBoost classifier with Bayesian hyperparameter optimization. SHAP values provide loan officer-interpretable explanations. Synthetic data augmentation addressed class imbalance.

title: Algorithmic Trading Strategy — Momentum + ML
description: Systematic long-short momentum strategy on NSE equities enhanced with ML signal generation. Uses cross-sectional momentum ranking, transaction cost modeling, and walk-forward optimization. Achieves positive Sharpe ratio over 2019-2023 backtest period.
longDescription: Strategy combines 3-12 month cross-sectional price momentum with fundamental quality screens. An XGBoost classifier filters high-confidence momentum signals. Portfolio construction uses volatility-scaled position sizing. The backtest framework is fully vectorized with realistic transaction costs (0.15% one-way), bid-ask spread estimation, and market impact modeling.

title: CFA Research Challenge — Equity Valuation Report
description: Full institutional-grade equity research report prepared for the CFA Research Challenge 2025 (East Africa). Includes DCF, EV/EBITDA, and P/E relative valuation, Porter's Five Forces, SWOT, and ESG analysis. Team finished 3rd Place nationally.
longDescription: Comprehensive buy-side style research report following CFA Institute standards. The valuation integrates a 5-year DCF with WACC decomposition, scenario analysis (bull/base/bear), and sensitivity tables. Comparable company analysis uses peer multiples from East African listed peers. The report includes macroeconomic overlay on Kenyan GDP, inflation, and interest rate forecasts from the CBK. Includes downloadable Excel financial model with valuation calculations.

title: ARDL-XGBOOST Hybrid model for Repo Rate Forecasting
description: Combining ARDL time series analysis with XGBoost regression to forecast Central Bank of Kenya repo rate movements.
longDescription: Combining ARDL time series analysis with XGBoost regression to forecast Central Bank of Kenya repo rate movements.

title: Rethink – AI-Powered Facultative Reinsurance Decision Support
description: AI-powered decision support system for facultative reinsurance that automates submission processing, risk analysis, and underwriting recommendations, transforming a slow manual workflow into a fast, intelligent, and transparent process.Team finished 4th and was reward 80,000 KES.
longDescription: REThink is an AI-powered facultative reinsurance decision support system developed during a hackathon organized by Kenya Reinsurance Corporation and University of Nairobi. The platform addresses inefficiencies in traditional facultative reinsurance workflows, where submissions arrive as scattered email attachments and underwriting decisions often take several days due to manual data extraction and inconsistent risk assessment. The system automates the end-to-end workflow from submission to decision through automated email ingestion, AI-powered document processing, and extraction of key risk information from unstructured documents. A rules-based engine evaluates risk factors such as catastrophe exposure, ESG considerations, loss ratios, and policy terms, while the AI decision engine generates recommendations for pricing or declining a risk with transparent reasoning and risk scoring. The platform provides dashboards for underwriters and supervisors to review decisions, maintain full audit trails, and monitor portfolio exposure. NOTE: Source code and implementation details are restricted due to NDA constraints with Kenya Reinsurance Corporation and cannot be publicly disclosed.

title: Melanin-Centered Skincare Recommendation System
description: AI-powered skincare recommendation system specifically designed for women of color, using machine learning to provide personalized product recommendations based on skin tone, type, and preferences. Moringa Data Science final project.
longDescription: This project addresses a significant gap in the skincare industry: the lack of tailored product recommendations for women of color, who often have unique skincare needs such as hyperpigmentation, dryness, and sensitivity. The system combines multiple AI techniques including content-based filtering (cosine similarity on ingredients), collaborative filtering (SVD), and sentiment analysis (LSTM model) to provide personalized recommendations. Built using data from over 8,000 Sephora products and 1 million user reviews, the system achieves high accuracy with Precision@1 of 0.99 and Hit Rate of 0.99. The Streamlit interface allows users to get recommendations based on product highlights, skin type, and skin tone preferences, specifically targeting melanin-rich skin concerns.
